message:
🐛 fix: address Copilot review on proxy 429 handling (#8307) (#8309)

Three follow-up items from PR #8304 review:

1. **Stale inline comment in Proxy()** said "30 req/min bucket" after the
   constant was raised to 60. Reworded to point at the constants instead
   of hard-coding a stale number, so future bumps don't require tracking
   down every comment.

2. **Missing Retry-After header on 429.** Other rate limiters in
   server.go already advertise Retry-After (#7040); the GitHub proxy
   was silent, so clients had to guess when to retry. Added
   githubProxyRetryAfterSeconds = 60 (matches the refill window) and
   set the header alongside the JSON error.

3. **Error body never reached the user.** The backend returns a helpful
   JSON message ("Console proxy rate limit exceeded (not GitHub)...")
   but GitHubActivity was throwing `response.statusText`, which for a
   429 is just "Too Many Requests" — the misleading text the bug report
   was about. Added githubFetchError() that prefers the JSON body's
   `error` field and falls back to statusText when the body is
   unavailable. Applied to all six fetch sites in the card.

Fixes: #8307

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
